mix.note: Base plus a utilization/margin-linked bonus rather than a sales-quota bonus; a commonly cited modeled split is roughly 70% base / 30% incentive at target utilization (only Roboflow's posting explicitly flags an OTE/bonus structure in this data, so the specific 70/30 split is an industry-typical assumption, not directly observed in the extracted numbers). Vendor in-house PS roles (Pendo, Greenhouse) show explicit equity mentions in the source, while pure consultancies and federal-adjacent roles (Reply, GDIT, AT&T) show none, consistent with base + utilization bonus and little or no equity.

caveat: Only 15 regex-extracted data points across 9 distinct companies (some duplicated across locations, e.g. Cisco, Pendo, Brex), spanning vendor professional-services roles, federal-adjacent consulting, and telecom/network consulting, so the range mixes fairly different comp models (pure base vs. an OTE-style posting from Roboflow) and 2 of the 15 figures (Greenhouse Ontario, Brex Vancouver) are in CAD rather than USD.

Data is regex-extracted from 36 postings across 31 companies with mixed currencies (some CAD/AUD figures not converted to USD) and inconsistent level_hint tagging (roughly half the postings have no seniority label, so the "Mid" bucket is inferred rather than explicit and includes some ranges — e.g. PlanetScale, Immuta — that may actually be senior-leaning); management/VP outliers (Boeing, CoreWeave, Salesforce) pull the top of the raw distribution well above the typical IC support-engineer band, so the top-level low/high above (65,000-200,000, from Fictiv and PlanetScale/Roboflow respectively) exclude those three postings to better represent the core individual-contributor role.

Data is regex-extracted from 25 postings across 18 companies (Harvey, Descope, SoundCloud, Block, Cloudflare, HP, Ping Identity, Highspot, Cisco, Fastly, Affirm, Veriff, Ibotta, Semgrep, Postman, Transmit Security, Vercel, Hightouch) with base ranges roughly $92K-$250K; level_hint values (manager/senior/sr) are parsed from job titles like "Technical Account Manager" rather than true seniority and overlap heavily (e.g. "manager"-tagged postings span $92K-$250K, overlapping "senior"/"sr"-tagged postings which together span $110K-$220K), so no clean level progression or base/bonus/equity percentage split could be reliably derived, and some high-end figures (e.g. Hightouch's $200K-$250K, Cisco's $241.7K) reflect on-target-earnings/commission-inclusive totals rather than pure base.

Sample is 80 regex-extracted salary lines from 525 scanned postings across 55 distinct companies (verified: unique-company count matches), mixing base-only and base+bonus+equity figures with inconsistent leveling language ("Associate," "Sr," "Lead," "Principal," "Manager," "Director" don't map to a single consistent ladder across employers). A level-bucket breakdown was attempted but dropped after verification: (1) Formation Bio's "Associate Director, Data Engineering" ($204.5K-$267K) was mis-bucketed into Entry/Junior purely because a regex matched the word "Associate," even though the role and pay are director-level; (2) Xometry was listed under Mid/Senior with no corresponding senior-labeled Xometry data point at all; (3) once bucket company lists were reconciled against their actual salary lines, ranges were internally contradicted by their own listed companies -- e.g. the Staff/Lead bucket (stated $131K-$233K) excluded Valtech's actual $110K floor and Attentive's actual $240K ceiling, both companies named in that bucket; the Principal/Director/Manager bucket (stated low $140K) excluded AT&T's actual $130.7K floor; and the Mid/Senior bucket, once corrected, spans roughly $82.7K (Pfizer) to $361K (Roku) -- a range that fully subsumes every other tier and provides no real separation. Given how noisy the leveling signal is, a per-level breakdown can't be presented without guessing category boundaries, so it has been set to null. Top-line low/high/typical are retained: low ($120,000, matches Varda Space Industries) and high ($245,000, matches Discord) both trace to real data points, though the true max in the sample is $361,000 (Roku) and $318,000 (Life360), which are treated as directional outliers -- consistent with this caveat. Read all figures as directional, not precise.

Sample is only 13 data points across 9 distinct companies (Temporal/Temporal Technologies counted once as a duplicate posting), level_hints are missing for 5 of 13 entries so a clean seniority-level breakdown isn't supported, and no posting or the existing prose gave a numeric base/bonus/equity percentage split, so mix is left null rather than invented.

Sample spans very different industry sub-verticals (aerospace/defense primes, consumer wearables, and VC-backed robotics/hard-tech startups) with wildly different pay philosophies, roughly 40% of postings had no parseable level_hint so the level buckets above only cover the ~15 points where seniority was explicit, and duplicate near-identical Whoop/WHOOP listings were not deduplicated in the raw counts.

Level bucketing is uneven (61 "manager"-tagged points vs. only 4 "director"-tagged), and since figures are regex-extracted from live postings, some ranges may reflect base-only vs. base+bonus text inconsistently or catch adjacent numbers (e.g., incentive-plan percentages) rather than pure salary.

Sample is 30 regex-extracted postings across 23 companies with no explicit base/bonus/equity percentage split disclosed anywhere, and about half the points carry no level_hint while at least one level_hint ("director" on the Zip posting) appears to be a false-positive pulled from stakeholder titles mentioned in the job text rather than the candidate's actual seniority, so level bucketing and the overall range should be read as directional, not precise. The overall range spans the full posted spread, from a Workato posting as low as $70,000 to a HackerRank Director-level posting as high as $350,000.

Sample skews heavily senior/staff (only 1 principal-level and no entry/junior-labeled postings, so the level breakdown excludes Principal and true early-career bands), and ranges are regex-extracted from job postings so a few outliers (e.g., a $50K low from a Bogota-based listing, excluded here) may reflect non-US pay bands rather than errors. Level bands (Senior/Staff/Lead) are the true min/max across all postings tagged with that level_hint, which is a wide spread because it mixes non-tech/insurance-sector postings (e.g., Caterpillar Senior at $112,710 low, Abbott Staff at $99,300 low) with well-compensated tech postings (e.g., Nextdoor Senior at $334,000 high, Salesforce Staff at $313,700 high); base/bonus/equity mix was left null because no posting in this dataset gave a clean numeric split.

Sample skews senior/staff (only one principal and no clearly junior/entry data points, and about a third of postings have no level_hint at all), so the range likely understates true entry-level pay and the level_hint field wasn't clean enough to build a reliable seniority ladder; figures are regex-extracted from live postings so a few ranges may reflect base-only vs. total-comp framing rather than a strict apples-to-apples base salary comparison.

Ranges are regex-extracted from live job postings across ~55 companies and 72 data points, so some multi-location postings (e.g. Pfizer) blend disparate metro cost-of-living bands, and a handful of AI/ML-infrastructure-flavored roles (Faire, CoreWeave, Hightouch) pull the senior/staff high end well above typical generic platform-engineering pay. Two extracted ranges also embed non-USD figures from multi-region postings (AT&T's Mexico City figure is MXN paid monthly, not USD annual; one Rumble posting's extracted range is its Canada/CAD band, not the US/USD one) — these distort direct dollar comparisons if not filtered out before aggregating.

Data is regex-extracted from live job-posting text across 63 companies and industries (big tech, insurance, retail, defense contractors), so ranges mix geographic tiers and full posting min-to-max bands rather than a single consistent leveling scale, and the "junior" and "principal" buckets rest on only 3-6 data points each so their edges are less reliable than the senior bucket's.

32 data points across 26 companies is a decent sample, but level_hint is null for most postings (only 15 of 32 are tagged senior/manager/principal, not 14), so the seniority breakdown is unreliable and some ranges mix base-only vs. OTE figures inconsistently across postings.

mix.note: Only HP's two postings state an explicit split (80%/20% salary/incentive OTE mix); no other posting in the source data quantifies a base/variable percentage split, so the earlier claim of a '70/30 or 80/20' pattern was not supported by this data and has been removed. Equity is mentioned in several postings (HP x2, Postman, LangChain x2, Column, Fireblocks, Decagon, Astronomer, Yugabyte) but never given as a percentage, so it is treated as 0% in this structured split.

caveat: Ranges are regex-extracted from 80 job-posting data points across 58 distinct companies, but roughly a third of postings lacked a clear seniority level_hint (bucketed out of the level breakdown), and government/defense-contractor postings (e.g., Palantir's Forward Deployed role, GDIT) sit meaningfully lower than commercial-track roles at the same nominal seniority, so the level bands blend two different pay scales.

Only 8 regex-extracted data points across 5 distinct companies (Reply, Valtech, Effectual, Caylent, GDIT), skewed toward consulting/SI and federal-contractor postings, so the range may not represent vendor-side or Big-4 solutions architect comp, and level_hints were too sparse (only 2 of 5 distinct labels had 2+ points) to support a reliable seniority-tier breakdown.

9 data points from 6 companies (Via, Zip, 3M, GDIT, Salesforce, Reply) with level_hint present on just one record (3M, 'senior'), so there isn't enough consistent seniority signal to build a level ladder. Ranges mix base-only figures and OTE figures rather than a clearly documented base/bonus/equity split, so no mix breakdown is given. The full observed range is wide (115K-285.8K) because it spans a lower-paying listing (Via, $115K-$140K) up to a big-metro Salesforce band ($207.8K-$285.8K); the ~$185K typical figure is the average of each posting's range midpoint across all 9 points.

Ranges are regex-extracted from live job postings across 29 distinct companies. The 'low'/'high' fields are true min/max across each bucket's data points (not trimmed), so a few unusually wide single postings (e.g. Roku's $148,750-$361,000 California band, Cisco's $163,600-$269,800 non-metro band) set the outer edges - actual typical offers cluster well inside these bounds (median midpoint ~$203,000). Sample sizes per level bucket are modest (7-18 points), and no posting broke out an explicit base/bonus/equity percentage split so total-comp mix could not be derived from this data alone.

Based on only 14 regex-extracted data points across 12 companies (Mastercard, Visa, Invoca, Freenome, Humana, Caterpillar, T-Mobile, Cisco, ClassDojo, Micron, Hasbro, 1X Technologies), so level-progression and base/bonus/equity split can't be reliably isolated from the noise, and a couple of senior/lead/principal postings (Micron $141K-$319K, 1X Technologies $250K-$300K) sit well above the bulk of the range and pull the ceiling up.
